mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment counts signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative employment shock, or the predictive t-statistic falls below -1.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business confidence, forcing equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its historical trend reflects binding supply constraints and capacity utilization shocks, which compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity forward returns over a six-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains positive while 60-day forward equity returns are consistently negative, breaking the fundamental earnings-price linkage.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy stances, elevating real discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which yields negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The inflation threshold triggers consistently precede positive equity returns, or the forward return differential between high and low inflation regimes shows no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when both metrics improve simultaneously, or the interaction term in a predictive regression is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real retail sales combined with falling housing permits signals a breakdown in aggregate demand, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The joint demand signal produces positive forward returns in the majority of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between the signal and subsequent equity returns falls below -0.10.
